meta.description: Intimacy building positions with three couple variants per position: him & her, her & her, him & him. Premium-only with weekly releases.
meta.releaseSchedule: 10 positions at launch (releaseWeek 0), then 1 new position per week
meta.inclusivity: Every position includes three variant descriptions so all couples see themselves represented. The default Partner A / Partner B setup remains as a universal fallback.

items.title: The Lotus
items.focus: A seated face-to-face position that prioritizes skin-to-skin closeness, sustained eye contact, and synchronized breathing.
items.setup: Partner A sits upright with legs extended. Partner B sits in their lap facing them, wrapping their legs around Partner A's waist. Partner B can sit upright for forehead-to-forehead closeness or arch back to open up the angle — Partner A's arms hold them securely either way.
items.whyItWorks: Maximizes closeness with minimal physical strain. The face-to-face intimacy slows the pace and deepens emotional connection. The arch option opens up the chest and shifts sensation while keeping full body contact.

variants.him-her: He sits upright with legs extended. She sits in his lap facing him, wrapping her legs around his waist. She can stay upright for eye contact or arch back while he holds her securely.
variants.her-her: One partner sits upright with legs extended. The other sits in her lap facing her, wrapping her legs around her waist. She can arch back or stay close — arms hold her either way.
variants.him-him: One partner sits upright with legs extended. The other sits in his lap facing him, wrapping his legs around his waist. He can arch back or stay close — arms hold him securely.

items.title: The Slow Melt
items.focus: Both partners sit together — one behind, one in front — with the front partner arching dramatically backward, face tilted to the ceiling, fully open and surrendered.
items.setup: Partner A sits with legs extended. Partner B sits between Partner A's legs facing away, then arches their upper body all the way backward — spine opening, face pointing toward the ceiling, weight supported by Partner A's arms and chest. Partner A holds Partner B secure through the arch.
items.whyItWorks: The dramatic arch opens the front partner's chest and throat completely, creating a feeling of total openness and surrender. The back partner feels the full weight and trust of being leaned into. Sensation is intense from the angle and the vulnerability of the open-back position.

variants.him-her: He sits with legs extended. She sits between his legs facing away, then arches her upper body all the way back — face toward the ceiling, chest open. He holds her through the arch.
variants.her-her: One partner sits with legs extended. The other sits between her legs facing away, then arches her upper body dramatically backward, face to the ceiling. Arms hold her through the arch.
variants.him-him: One partner sits with legs extended. The other sits between his legs facing away, then arches his upper body all the way back — face up, chest open. Arms hold him through the arch.

items.title: The Compass
items.focus: Partner on top faces away, offering a completely different angle and sense of independence while staying deeply connected.
items.setup: Partner A lies back comfortably. Partner B straddles them facing their feet, sitting fully upright. Hands can rest on Partner A's legs or the bed for balance.
items.whyItWorks: The reversed angle changes sensation entirely. The top partner sits upright and controls the pace and depth, while the view and feeling for both is completely fresh.

variants.him-her: He lies back. She straddles him facing his feet, sitting upright. Her hands rest on his legs or the bed for balance.
variants.her-her: One partner lies back. The other straddles her facing her feet, sitting upright. Hands rest on her partner's legs for balance. Use toys as desired.
variants.him-him: One partner lies back. The other straddles him facing his feet, sitting upright. Hands rest on his partner's legs for balance.

items.title: The Harbor
items.focus: A classic from-behind position on all fours that offers deep connection through rhythm and trust.
items.setup: Partner B positions themselves on hands and knees. Partner A kneels behind them, hands resting on Partner B's hips or lower back. Both find a shared rhythm.
items.whyItWorks: The angle allows deep physical connection while the from-behind position builds trust. Partner A can lean forward for closeness or stay upright for control.

variants.him-her: She positions herself on hands and knees. He kneels behind her, hands on her hips or lower back. Both find a shared rhythm.
variants.her-her: One partner is on hands and knees. The other kneels behind her, hands on her hips. Use hands or toys to connect. Both find a shared rhythm.
variants.him-him: One partner is on hands and knees. The other kneels behind him, hands on his hips or lower back. Both find a shared rhythm.

items.title: The Bridge
items.focus: One partner leans back on their hands with hips lifted while the other straddles and arches — a dynamic, connected position that takes intimacy somewhere unexpected.
items.setup: Partner A sits and leans back on both hands, lifting their hips off the ground (reverse tabletop position). Partner B straddles them facing forward, and can sit upright or arch their own body back dramatically. Both find their balance together.
items.whyItWorks: The reverse tabletop angle lifts the hips into a unique position that changes the feel entirely. Partner B's freedom to arch back adds intensity and a sense of abandon — it's physically engaging for both partners.

variants.him-her: He leans back on his hands, lifting his hips into a reverse tabletop. She straddles him and can sit upright or arch back dramatically — both find their balance together.
variants.her-her: One partner leans back on her hands with hips lifted. The other straddles her, sitting upright or arching back. Use hands or toys. Both find their balance.
variants.him-him: One partner leans back on his hands with hips lifted. The other straddles him and can sit upright or arch back. Both bodies engage together.

items.title: The Arc
items.focus: The top partner straddles facing away, then leans all the way back — chest to chest, completely surrendered.
items.setup: Partner A lies back. Partner B straddles them facing away, then slowly leans back until their back rests against Partner A's chest. Partner A's arms wrap around Partner B from behind. Both bodies settle into a full-length embrace.
items.whyItWorks: The lean-back angle transforms a familiar position into something unexpectedly close. The chest-to-back contact wraps around the top partner entirely, and the angle creates deep, satisfying pressure.

variants.him-her: He lies back. She straddles him facing away, then leans back into his chest. His arms wrap around her from behind.
variants.her-her: One partner lies back. The other straddles her facing away, then leans back. Arms wrap around from behind. Use hands or toys.
variants.him-him: One partner lies back. The other straddles him facing away, then leans back into his chest. Arms wrap around from behind.

items.title: The Overlap
items.focus: Both partners stack together on all fours — the bottom partner low with forearms down and head resting on their arms, the top partner kneeling over them, bodies fully aligned.
items.setup: Partner B gets on their knees and lowers down to their forearms, head resting on their folded arms. Partner A kneels over them from behind, chest pressing toward Partner B's back, hands on either side. Both bodies stack in alignment from knees to shoulders.
items.whyItWorks: The bottom partner's lowered forearm position changes the angle and creates a deeper, more enveloping connection. The full-body overlay from the top partner creates maximum closeness and a primal sense of being completely covered.

variants.him-her: She lowers to her forearms, head resting on her arms. He kneels over her from behind, chest toward her back, hands on either side of her.
variants.her-her: One partner lowers to her forearms, head down. The other kneels over her from behind, chest toward her back. Use hands or toys in the stacked position.
variants.him-him: One partner lowers to his forearms, head resting on his arms. The other kneels over him from behind, chest toward his back, hands on either side.

items.title: The Throne
items.focus: The partner on top straddles and rides, fully controlling the pace, angle, and depth while the other surrenders to their rhythm.
items.setup: Partner A lies on their back. Partner B straddles them, sitting upright. Partner B controls all the movement — speed, depth, and angle. Partner A's hands are free to roam.
items.whyItWorks: Gives the top partner complete control, which is empowering. The bottom partner practices surrender and learns to receive. Eye contact is easy and natural.

variants.him-her: He lies on his back. She straddles him sitting upright, controlling speed, depth, and angle. His hands are free to roam her body.
variants.her-her: One partner lies on her back. The other straddles her, sitting upright, controlling the rhythm. Use hands or toys. Eye contact is easy and natural.
variants.him-him: One partner lies on his back. The other straddles him, sitting upright, controlling speed and depth. Hands are free to explore.

items.title: The Drift
items.focus: A slow, close from-behind position — one partner lying flat while the other kneels over them from behind, body curled forward and close. Unhurried, low-effort, and deeply intimate.
items.setup: Partner B lies flat on their stomach. Partner A kneels over them from behind, leaning forward with arms around Partner B, body curled low and close. There's minimal movement — it's about sustained warmth and the feeling of being completely covered.
items.whyItWorks: The kneeling-over position lets the top partner stay close and enveloping without full body weight on the bottom partner. Perfect for slow, sleepy mornings or winding down at night — all closeness, no effort.

variants.him-her: She lies flat on her stomach. He kneels over her from behind, leaning forward with arms around her, body curled close. Slow, minimal movement — closeness over pace.
variants.her-her: One partner lies flat on her stomach. The other kneels over her from behind, leaning forward with arms around her. Use hands or toys. Slow and close.
variants.him-him: One partner lies flat on his stomach. The other kneels over him from behind, leaning forward with arms around him, body curled close. Slow, minimal movement.

items.title: The Pulse
items.focus: The classic face-to-face position — one partner on top, chest to chest, with full eye contact and kiss access.
items.setup: Partner B lies on their back. Partner A positions on top, chest to chest, weight supported on forearms. Faces are close enough to kiss, whisper, and maintain eye contact throughout.
items.whyItWorks: The most intimate position for a reason — full frontal contact, easy eye contact, natural kissing distance. Slowing it down and focusing on breath turns the familiar into something profound.

variants.him-her: She lies on her back. He positions on top, chest to chest, weight on his forearms. Close enough to kiss and maintain eye contact.
variants.her-her: One partner lies on her back. The other positions on top, chest to chest. Use hands or toys. Close enough to kiss and hold eye contact.
variants.him-him: One partner lies on his back. The other positions on top, chest to chest, weight on forearms. Close enough to kiss and hold eye contact.

items.title: The Reveal
items.focus: One partner lifts the other completely off the ground — face to face, both fully present and committed to each other.
items.setup: Partner A stands firmly and lifts Partner B, who wraps their legs tightly around Partner A's waist and arms around their shoulders. Partner A supports Partner B's weight with both hands. A wall behind Partner A can help with balance.
items.whyItWorks: Being held off the ground creates immediate vulnerability and complete trust. The face-to-face closeness at eye level amplifies every sensation — nowhere to look but at each other.

variants.him-her: He lifts her completely, hands supporting her. She wraps her legs around his waist and arms around his shoulders. A wall behind him helps with balance.
variants.her-her: One partner lifts the other completely. The lifted partner wraps legs around her waist and arms around her shoulders. Use a wall for support.
variants.him-him: One partner lifts the other completely off the ground. The lifted partner wraps legs around his waist and holds tight. A wall behind helps with balance.

items.title: The Anchor
items.focus: One partner is on all fours with one leg raised and supported — a rear-entry position with a uniquely deep and open angle.
items.setup: Partner B positions on all fours at the edge of a bed or surface. They raise one leg upward — nearly vertical — which Partner A (kneeling behind) supports with both hands. The elevated leg creates a deep, open angle.
items.whyItWorks: Raising the leg straight up dramatically opens the hip and shifts the angle to one of the deepest available in a rear-entry position. The vertical leg gives the kneeling partner control over depth and angle by adjusting how high they hold it.

variants.him-her: She gets on all fours at the bed's edge and raises one leg straight up. He kneels behind, supporting her raised leg with both hands. The vertical leg controls the depth and angle.
variants.her-her: One partner gets on all fours and raises one leg upward. The other kneels behind, supporting it with both hands. Use hands or toys. Adjust height to control the angle.
variants.him-him: One partner gets on all fours and raises one leg straight up. The other kneels behind, holding the raised leg with both hands. The vertical angle deepens the connection.

items.title: The Constellation
items.focus: One partner lies at the edge of a bed or surface — upper body hanging off the edge with hands on the floor — while the other stands between their legs. A gravity-driven angle unlike anything on a flat surface.
items.setup: Partner B lies on their back with hips right at the edge of the bed. Their upper body hangs off the edge, arms reaching down toward the floor for support. Partner A stands between their legs, holding Partner B's hips or thighs to keep them steady.
items.whyItWorks: The inverted upper body creates a unique blood-rush and heightened sensitivity. The standing partner has full control while the lying partner is in a completely open, surrendered, head-down position — an intense combination.

variants.him-her: She lies with hips at the bed edge, upper body hanging off with hands toward the floor. He stands between her legs, holding her hips steady.
variants.her-her: One partner lies with hips at the edge, upper body hanging off. The other stands between her legs, holding her hips. Use hands or toys.
variants.him-him: One partner lies with hips at the bed edge, upper body hanging off with hands toward the floor. The other stands between his legs, holding his hips steady.

items.title: The Gravity Well
items.focus: One partner wraps their legs around the other's hips while standing — pressed completely together, face-to-face, nowhere to go but closer.
items.setup: Partner A stands firmly with arms ready to hold. Partner B steps in close, wraps their legs around Partner A's hips, and holds on with their arms around Partner A's neck or shoulders. Both faces are at the same level — close enough to kiss with every movement.
items.whyItWorks: The full-body press brings everything into contact at once. Both partners are completely upright and present, faces level, nothing between them. The wrap creates a sense of being fully held while staying completely connected.

variants.him-her: He stands firmly. She wraps her legs around his hips and arms around his neck. Both faces are level, close enough to kiss throughout.
variants.her-her: One partner stands firmly. The other wraps her legs around her partner's hips and holds on. Use hands or toys. Both faces stay close together.
variants.him-him: One partner stands firmly. The other wraps his legs around his hips and holds on with arms around his neck or shoulders. Faces stay close.

items.title: The Quiet Storm
items.focus: One partner drops from all fours to their forearms — lowering the angle and intensifying the depth while staying completely supported.
items.setup: Partner B starts on all fours, then lowers to their forearms, forehead resting on folded arms. Partner A kneels behind. The lowered angle changes the geometry entirely — deeper, fuller, more surrendered.
items.whyItWorks: Dropping to forearms shifts the angle to one of the deepest available in a rear-entry position. It requires no extra flexibility — just a willingness to let go and sink in.

variants.him-her: She drops to her forearms, forehead resting on her arms. He kneels behind. The lower angle intensifies depth and closeness.
variants.her-her: One partner drops to her forearms. The other kneels behind, using hands or toys. The lowered position creates a deep, surrendered angle.
variants.him-him: One partner drops to his forearms, head resting on his arms. The other kneels behind. The lowered angle intensifies the depth.

items.title: The Shelter
items.focus: Both partners settle into a semi-reclined position side by side — one close behind the other, bodies angled and open, deeply relaxed together.
items.setup: Partner A sits and leans back slightly, legs extended or loosely bent. Partner B reclines against Partner A at a perpendicular angle — their bodies forming a rough L-shape, connected at the hips. Partner A's hands rest on Partner B's hips or thighs. Both bodies settle without needing to hold anything up.
items.whyItWorks: The semi-reclined angle is effortless for both partners — no one holds their weight up. The bodies naturally settle together, creating a relaxed closeness that lets both fully let go and feel each other.

variants.him-her: He leans back slightly with legs extended. She reclines against him at an angle, his hands on her hips or thighs. Both sink into the position together.
variants.her-her: One partner leans back with legs extended. The other reclines against her at an angle. Use hands or toys. Both relax into each other fully.
variants.him-him: One partner leans back with legs extended. The other reclines against him at an angle, his hands on his partner's hips. Both settle in together.

items.title: The Pendulum
items.focus: One partner bends all the way forward — hands near the floor, hips high — while the other kneels in close from behind. Intense, deep, and completely surrendered.
items.setup: Partner B bends forward as far as they can, hands on the floor for support, hips elevated. Partner A kneels behind them, hands gripping Partner B's hips or waist. The extreme forward angle creates a deep, gravity-assisted connection.
items.whyItWorks: Bending all the way forward shifts the body into a steep downward angle that intensifies everything. Partner A's kneeling position keeps them stable and fully in control while Partner B is completely open and surrendered.

variants.him-her: She bends all the way forward, hands on the floor for support, hips high. He kneels behind, hands on her hips. The steep angle intensifies everything.
variants.her-her: One partner bends far forward with hands on the floor. The other kneels behind, using hands or toys. The steep angle creates deep, open access.
variants.him-him: One partner bends all the way forward, hands on the floor. The other kneels behind, hands on his hips or waist. The extreme angle intensifies the connection.
